skill.name: copywriting
skill.description: Write, rewrite, and improve marketing page copy for homepages, landing pages, pricing pages, feature pages, about pages, and product pages.
skill.summary: Create clear conversion-focused copy for marketing pages.
skill.author: coreyhaines31

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported 16 suspicious patterns, but review found they are false positives from marketing and copywriting language in SKILL.md. No command execution, network calls, filesystem access, environment access, credential handling, or prompt injection attempts were found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: The flagged lines contain copywriting terms, page structure guidance, or metadata labels. They do not reference cryptographic algorithms or executable code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Threat confidence is very low because the lines are natural-language copywriting guidance. No cryptographic API, hashing function, or code execution path is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The flagged lines advise avoiding jargon and vague marketing words. They do not collect system data or inspect host configuration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Threat confidence is very low because the context is writing style guidance. There is no request to enumerate files, users, processes, or machine details.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Network Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The flagged lines discuss clarity, page traffic sources, and simple process steps. They do not scan networks, open sockets, or call external services.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Threat confidence is very low because the evidence is plain marketing instruction. No network target, scanning command, or network library usage appears.

content.value_statement: Many teams know what they sell but struggle to explain it clearly. This skill turns product context into structured, persuasive page copy.
actual_capabilities: Drafts page copy for homepages, landing pages, pricing pages, feature pages, product pages, and about pages.
Creates headlines, subheadlines, calls to action, section headers, body copy, and secondary CTAs.
Uses audience, product, traffic source, objections, and proof points to shape the message.
Provides alternative headline and CTA options with brief rationale.
Applies conversion copy principles such as clarity, specificity, benefits, objections, and social proof.
limitations: It depends on accurate product, audience, and proof point inputs from the user.
It does not verify claims, statistics, customer quotes, or legal compliance.
It is focused on web page copy, not full email sequences or popup strategy.
It does not create visual designs, wireframes, analytics plans, or A/B test implementations.

use_cases.title: Launch a Landing Page
use_cases.description: Turn campaign context into a focused landing page with a single message and clear CTA.

use_cases.title: Improve a Homepage
use_cases.description: Rewrite vague homepage copy into clearer sections for several visitor intents.

use_cases.title: Clarify a Feature Page
use_cases.description: Connect product features to customer outcomes with benefits, proof, objections, and CTA options.

prompt_templates.title: Draft a Basic Page
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner prompt for a first complete draft.
prompt_templates.prompt: Write copy for a landing page. Product: [product]. Audience: [audience]. Main action: [CTA]. Include headline, subheadline, sections, and CTA.

prompt_templates.title: Rewrite Existing Copy
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate prompt for improving a current page.
prompt_templates.prompt: Rewrite this page copy for clarity and conversion. Keep the offer accurate. Audience: [audience]. Objections: [objections]. Existing copy: [paste copy].

prompt_templates.title: Build a Full Page Narrative
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced prompt for a structured page argument.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a homepage narrative for [product]. Include audience context, pain, benefits, proof, objections, section flow, headline options, and CTA options.

prompt_templates.title: Optimize for Campaign Traffic
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced prompt for message matching a traffic source.
prompt_templates.prompt: Write landing page copy for traffic from [source]. Match the ad promise: [promise]. Include problem, solution, proof, FAQ, and final CTA.

output_examples.input: A founder needs homepage copy for a reporting tool that saves teams time.
output_examples.output: The skill returns a homepage structure with a clear headline, outcome-focused subheadline, benefit sections, proof prompts, and CTA alternatives.

output_examples.input: A marketer has vague feature page copy for an analytics product.
output_examples.output: The skill rewrites feature descriptions into customer outcomes, adds objection handling, and suggests stronger CTA text.

output_examples.input: A team needs landing page copy for paid search traffic.
output_examples.output: The skill creates a focused page with message-matched headline, pain section, solution section, social proof, FAQ, and final CTA.

best_practices: Provide audience, offer, main action, proof points, objections, and traffic source before asking for copy.
Ask for several headline and CTA alternatives when the page has high business impact.
Review every statistic, testimonial, guarantee, and product claim before publishing.
anti_patterns: Do not ask it to invent metrics, testimonials, customer names, or legal guarantees.
Do not use generic audience labels when real customer language is available.
Do not use it as a substitute for design, analytics, legal review, or customer research.

faq.question: What pages can this skill help write?
faq.answer: It supports homepages, landing pages, pricing pages, feature pages, product pages, and about pages.

faq.question: Can it improve existing page copy?
faq.answer: Yes. Provide the current copy, audience, goal, objections, and proof points for a focused rewrite.

faq.question: Does it write email campaigns?
faq.answer: No. The skill points users to a separate email-sequence skill for email copy.

faq.question: Does it verify marketing claims?
faq.answer: No. Users must verify statistics, testimonials, guarantees, and product claims before publishing.

faq.question: Can it provide multiple copy options?
faq.answer: Yes. It can provide headline and CTA alternatives with brief rationale.

faq.question: Is this compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
